surface.name: Getting Started on Heroku with Node.js sample deploy
surface.selection_basis: Heroku Dev Center Getting Started with Node.js is the general Cedar sample deploy: CLI login, clone official sample, heroku create, git push heroku main, open the herokuapp.com URL. Language peers exist; Node.js is a complete official path.

alternatives_considered.name: Other language getting-started guides
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Peer language samples; Node.js is a fully documented representative Cedar path.

alternatives_considered.name: Fir Private Spaces guide
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Fir is for Private Spaces; tutorial targets Cedar.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Heroku account, deploy the official Node.js sample, and open the app URL.
assumed_prior_state: Git and Heroku CLI can be installed.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The Node.js sample is running on a Heroku dyno and loads at its herokuapp.com URL.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Browser shows the sample app content after heroku open or visiting the app URL.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Getting Started deploys via git push heroku and opens the app on the platform hostname.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: First Heroku success is a live sample URL, not CLI login alone.

prerequisites.requirement: Dyno usage may incur charges; tutorial recommends low-cost plans.

primary_path.action: Install the Heroku CLI and Git.
Run heroku login and complete browser authentication.
Run heroku create --stack heroku-26 to create the app and git remote.
primary_path.success_signal: App URL and git.heroku.com remote printed.

primary_path.action: Deploy with git push heroku main (or master as documented).
Open the app with heroku open and confirm the sample site.

friction_gates.description: Dyno/database usage counts toward account usage.

sources.title: Getting Started on Heroku with Node.js
